beast_pools.trs: “Well, in the 1950s, detective work was often concentrated in the poorer sections of cities, typically heavily populated by Italian, Irish, and Polish immigrants, and of course, African American neighborhoods in the segregated South and even in Northern cities. The crimes were usually 'domestic' – things like petty theft, gambling, and brawls...People in those areas tended to be dressed in worn clothing, always looking over their shoulder...”
